title: tragia volubilis var. triangularis
intro: Tragia volubilis var. triangularis is a plant species found in tropical regions, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is commonly known as the "Small Cordia" or "Wild Bitter Apple" due to its small size and bitter taste. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Tragia volubilis var. triangularis can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to boost the immune system and provide relief from colds and flu.
